limitations: Operator chat timing inside this slice is approximate unless anchored to repo receipts.
The final package report for 0.6.18 records build inventory only and was regenerated with -SkipVarDistribute so the live authority remained raw dev_deploy only.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: The operator chose to move directly to a new alpha and requested 0.6.18 for immediate testing rather than waiting on a 0.6.17 manual pass.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: The runtime review concluded that fps and audio kHz are not reconciled directly; video and audio must both follow one master clock, and the strongest remaining code seam after 0.6.17 was the VideoPlayer posture of skipOnDrop = false under load.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Chose the narrowest safe next fix: keep the retired periodic hard-correction seam disabled, leave transport and focus behavior alone, and change only the VideoPlayer recovery posture so late frames may be dropped instead of building visible catch-up backlog.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Built 0.6.18 cleanly and stamped the release boundary before redeploying the raw alpha artifacts.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Identified that -PackageOnlyDeploy was the wrong raw-only inventory flag, regenerated the 0.6.18 package report with -SkipVarDistribute, and confirmed that the final live authority stayed raw dev_deploy only without leaving a competing .var in AddonPackages.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Refreshed the baseline direct-player raw asset to the same 0.6.18 alpha core so the operator can compare the generic player baseline and hosted proofs on one coherent dev_deploy slice.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Redeployed the raw modern_tv hosted-player proof as the canonical 0.6.18 alpha dev_deploy seam after changing the VideoPlayer frame-drop posture under load.

decisions: 0.6.18 is its own semantic version because the runtime playback behavior changed after 0.6.17 had already been built for testing.
The current live authority seam for this slice remains raw dev_deploy Custom artifacts, not the .var package.
The once-per-second active AV correction seek remains retired for the current stability lane.
The next narrow experiment after 0.6.17 is to allow late frames to drop under load rather than cling to visible catch-up backlog.
Existing seek resume, focus resume, resize rollback, preset behavior, and current control contracts remain intact in this slice.
openQuestions: Does 0.6.18 materially reduce or remove the periodic stutter/catch-up seam on the same problematic movie tests?
If stutter remains, is the next safer experiment to adopt a different Unity clocking model rather than continue changing recovery posture?
How much worse does the seam become when multiple active instances are present versus a single active player?
notes: The 0.6.18 package report exists because the hosted proof script expects package inventory, and the final report was regenerated with -SkipVarDistribute so the live authority did not drift away from raw dev_deploy.
This slice changes recovery posture under load but does not add a new active sync algorithm.
